message:
chore(asupersync): realign owner_bead references, re-baseline invariant counts, and add ratchet schedule

Governance updates for the ASUPERSYNC Tokio migration program:

- Realign all owner_bead references from legacy bead IDs (235t.*) to
  current live beads (1ud0u.*) across all 25+ crate-level dependency
  exception entries and the program epic reference
- Re-baseline source invariant max_count values to measured actuals
  reflecting migration progress (e.g., tokio_refs_total 466→159,
  tokio_test_attributes 314→109, tokio_runtime_builder 19→1,
  tokio_spawn_usage 16→4, tokio_select_usage 6→2)
- Add complete ratchet_schedule with 5 phased waves (Phase 5 Core Crate
  through Phase 10 Final Cutover) spanning 2026-03-10 to 2026-03-21,
  each with explicit checkpoint dates and decrement targets
- Include escalation policy with 2-day slip threshold and block/notify
  behavior
- Update bead tracker status for completed guardrail sub-tasks
  (1ud0u.4.3 and 1ud0u.4.4)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Create the canonical migration contract for this program so every subsequent bead is executable and auditable without external markdown docs.

- Enumerate all direct Tokio coupling forms in this repository:

  - source references (`tokio::`, `#[tokio::test]`, runtime builders/spawn/select/time/sync),
  - runtime macro entrypoints.
- Define exact replacement map to ASUPERSYNC/fcp-async-core APIs by category.
- Define correctness invariants for cancellation, deadlines, retries, and shutdown.
- Define migration wave order by blast radius and risk.

## Required Artifacts
1. Migration matrix table (crate/connector -> remaining Tokio surfaces -> owner bead -> exit criteria).
2. API replacement matrix (old symbol -> new symbol -> semantic caveats).
3. Semantic test matrix (what must be proven after each migration).
4. Program ratchet policy (what checks get tightened and when).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Matrix covers every crate/connector currently using Tokio directly.
- Every listed surface has a concrete replacement strategy.
- All downstream phase beads can reference this bead as authoritative contract.

## 4. Direct Bypass Offenders (use tokio:: instead of fcp_async_core)

## 1. Runtime Bootstrap

| Tokio API | Replacement | Module | Semantic Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `#[tokio::main]` | `#[fcp_async_core::runtime::main]` | runtime | Already re-exported. Identical behavior. |
| `#[tokio::test]` | `#[fcp_async_core::runtime::test]` | runtime | Already re-exported. Identical behavior. Drop-in replacement. |
| `tokio::runtime::Runtime::new()` | `fcp_async_core::runtime::Runtime::new()` | runtime | Re-exported. For sync-context bridge use `block_on_sync()` instead. |
| `tokio::runtime::Builder::new_...()` | `fcp_async_core::runtime::Builder::new_...()` | runtime | Re-exported. Prefer ConnectorRuntime for connectors. |
| Direct runtime construction | `ConnectorRuntime::new(config)` | fcp-sdk/migration | **Preferred** for connectors. Provides execution contexts, shutdown, health. |

**Risky substitution:** None — all are drop-in re-exports or direct improvements.

## 2. Task Spawn/Join

| Tokio API | Replacement | Module | Semantic Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tokio::spawn(fut)` | `fcp_async_core::task::spawn(fut)` | task | Re-exported. Identical semantics. |
| `tokio::task::JoinHandle<T>` | `fcp_async_core::task::JoinHandle<T>` | task | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::task::yield_now()` | `fcp_async_core::task::yield_now()` | task | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::task::block_in_place()` | `fcp_async_core::runtime::block_on_sync()` | runtime | **BEHAVIORAL CHANGE**: block_on_sync checks runtime flavor, falls back to current-thread builder. Cancellation-aware. |
| Ad-hoc spawn + join | `TaskGroup::spawn(name, fut)` | fcp-async-core | **Preferred**. Structured shutdown + instrumentation hooks. |

**Risky substitution:** `block_in_place` → `block_on_sync`: different error paths. Test that sync-context callers handle AsyncError::Runtime.

## 3. Select

| Tokio API | Replacement | Module | Semantic Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tokio::select\! { ... }` | `fcp_async_core::select\! { ... }` | root | Direct re-export. Identical semantics. |

**Risky substitution:** None — literal re-export.

## 4. Time/Deadline

| Tokio API | Replacement | Module | Semantic Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tokio::time::sleep(dur)` | `fcp_async_core::time::sleep(dur)` | time | Wrapper. Returns same Sleep type. |
| `tokio::time::timeout(dur, fut)` | `fcp_async_core::time::timeout(dur, fut)` | time | **BEHAVIORAL CHANGE**: Returns `Result<T, AsyncError::Timeout>` instead of `Result<T, Elapsed>`. Callers must match on `AsyncError`. |
| `tokio::time::interval(per)` | `fcp_async_core::time::interval(per)` | time | Wrapper. Returns same Interval type. |
| `tokio::time::Interval` | `fcp_async_core::time::Interval` | time | Re-exported type. |
| `tokio::time::Sleep` | `fcp_async_core::time::Sleep` | time | Re-exported type. |
| Manual deadline tracking | `Deadline::after(dur)` + `deadline.run(fut)` | fcp-async-core | **Preferred**. Composable with ExecutionContext. |
| Request timeout pattern | `ExecutionContext::request_scoped(dur).run(fut)` | fcp-async-core | **Preferred**. Combines timeout + cancellation. |

**Risky substitution:** `timeout()` error type change: all callers matching on `Elapsed` must switch to `AsyncError::Timeout`. Extra test: verify timeout_ms field accuracy.

## 5. Sync Primitives / Channels

| Tokio API | Replacement | Module | Semantic Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tokio::sync::mpsc::channel(cap)` | `fcp_async_core::channel::bounded(name, cap)` | channel | **BEHAVIORAL CHANGE**: Returns `BoundedSender/BoundedReceiver` with instrumentation hooks. Errors map to `AsyncError` not `SendError`. |
| `tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<T>` | `fcp_async_core::channel::BoundedSender<T>` | channel | Different error types. |
| `tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<T>` | `fcp_async_core::channel::BoundedReceiver<T>` | channel | Compatible behavior. |
| `tokio::sync::broadcast` | `fcp_async_core::channel::broadcast` | channel | Direct re-export. |
| `tokio::sync::watch` | `fcp_async_core::channel::watch` | channel | Direct re-export. |
| `tokio::sync::oneshot` | No direct re-export | — | **GAP**: fcp-async-core does not re-export oneshot. Needed by slack tests. Options: (a) add re-export, (b) use watch channel. |
| `tokio::sync::Mutex` | `fcp_async_core::sync::Mutex` | sync | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::sync::RwLock` | `fcp_async_core::sync::RwLock` | sync | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::sync::Semaphore` | `fcp_async_core::sync::Semaphore` | sync | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::sync::OwnedSemaphorePermit` | `fcp_async_core::sync::OwnedSemaphorePermit` | sync | Re-exported. |

**Risky substitutions:**
- mpsc channel: error types change. All `SendError`/`TrySendError` matches → `AsyncError::ChannelClosed`/`AsyncError::ChannelFull`.
- oneshot: NOT re-exported. Must decide: add re-export to fcp-async-core or replace with watch.

## 6. IO / Process / Net

| Tokio API | Replacement | Module | Semantic Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tokio::io::AsyncBufReadExt` | `fcp_async_core::io::AsyncBufReadExt` | io | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::io::AsyncReadExt` | `fcp_async_core::io::AsyncReadExt` | io | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::io::AsyncWriteExt` | `fcp_async_core::io::AsyncWriteExt` | io | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::io::BufReader` | `fcp_async_core::io::BufReader` | io | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::process::Command` | `fcp_async_core::process::Command` | process | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::process::Child` | `fcp_async_core::process::Child` | process | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::process::ChildStdin` | `fcp_async_core::process::ChildStdin` | process | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::process::ChildStdout` | `fcp_async_core::process::ChildStdout` | process | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::process::ChildStderr` | `fcp_async_core::process::ChildStderr` | process | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::net::TcpListener` | `fcp_async_core::net::TcpListener` | net | Re-exported. |
| `tokio::net::TcpStream` | `fcp_async_core::net::TcpStream` | net | Re-exported. |

**Risky substitution:** None — all are direct re-exports.

## 7. tokio-tungstenite (WebSocket)

| Tokio API | Replacement | Module | Semantic Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tokio_tungstenite::connect_async()` | TBD — fcp-streaming websocket | fcp-streaming | **GAP**: No async-core abstraction for WebSocket connect. fcp-streaming wraps it but connectors still import directly. |
| `tokio_tungstenite::MaybeTlsStream` | TBD | fcp-streaming | **GAP**: Type alias or re-export needed in fcp-streaming. |
| `tokio_tungstenite::WebSocketStream` | TBD | fcp-streaming | **GAP**: Type alias or re-export needed. |

**Risky substitution:** WebSocket abstraction doesn't exist yet. Must be created in fcp-streaming or fcp-async-core. Affects: discord (gateway), slack (websocket), fcp-streaming, fcp-graphql.

## 8. tokio-stream

| Tokio API | Replacement | Module | Semantic Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tokio_stream::Stream` (trait) | `futures_util::Stream` | futures-util | Standard trait. No tokio dep needed. |
| `tokio_stream::iter()` | `futures_util::stream::iter()` | futures-util | Standard replacement. |
| `tokio_stream::wrappers::ReceiverStream` | Build from mpsc with `BoundedReceiver` | fcp-async-core | **GAP**: No ReceiverStream wrapper. Must create or use futures-util StreamExt. |

**Risky substitution:** ReceiverStream — must create adapter or inline pattern. Affects: fcp-graphql subscriptions.

## 9. Retry & Error Patterns (ConnectorRuntime)

Also dependency updated to 1.1 to reduce unnecessary serialization while retaining rigor.","created_at":"2026-03-03T20:54:08Z"},{"id":596,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-1ud0u.1.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"COMPLETED: Migration Wave Sequencing + Risk/Blast-Radius Plan

## Wave Ordering (Critical Path)

```
Phase 0 (Charter)  ──┬── 1.1 ✓ Inventory
                      ├── 1.2 ✓ Replacement Matrix  
                      └── 1.3 ✓ This plan
                            │
Phase 1 (Substrate) ──┬── 2.1 Integrate /dp/asupersync → fcp-async-core
                      └── 2.2 Remove Tokio-leaking re-exports
                            │
                   ┌────────┼────────┬───────────┐
                   │        │        │           │
Phase 1B ─── 3.x Correctness    2.3 Macros   2.4 Parity
                   │        │        │
Phase 2 ──── 4.x Guardrails ✓    │  │
                            │     │  │
Phase 3 (Core) ──── 5.x ───┤     │  │
                   (8 crates)     │  │
                            │     │  │
Phase 4 (Infra) ── 6.x ────┤   7.x Test Harness
                   (7 crates)     │  (via 2.3 → parallel)
                            │     │
Phase 5 (Conn-A) ─ 8.x ────┤     │
                   (21 connectors)│
                            │     │
Phase 6 (Conn-B) ─ 9.x ────┤     │
                   (6 streaming)  │
                            │     │
Phase 7 (Final) ── 10.x ───┘─────┘
```

## Parallelization Lanes

**Lane A (Critical Path)**: 2.1 → 2.2 → 5.x → 6.x → 9.x → 10.x
- Longest chain. All infrastructure must flow through substrate hardening.
- 5.x crates (fcp-core, fcp-sdk, etc.) block 8.x connectors.
- 6.x infra (streaming, graphql) blocks 9.x streaming connectors.

**Lane B (Test Harness — parallel after 2.3)**: 2.3 → 7.x
- Test macro strategy (2.3) depends only on 2.2 (substrate).
- 7.x (testkit, conformance, e2e, cli) can run in parallel with 5.x/6.x.
- 7.5 (cross-workspace E2E) should wait until 5.x/6.x complete.

**Lane C (Correctness — parallel after 2.2)**: 3.x
- Cancellation (3.1) and deadline (3.2) tests depend on 2.2.
- 3.3 (differential regression) depends on 3.1 + 3.2.
- 6.x infra wave requires 3.3 as a gate.

**Lane D (Guardrails — already done)**: 4.x ✓
- 4.1 ✓ Ledger remapping, 4.2 ✓ Re-baseline, 4.3 ✓ Ratchet schedule
- 4.4 (CI enforcement) can proceed independently.

## Intra-Wave Parallelism

### Phase 3 — Core (5.x): 8 crates, all share 2.2 as prerequisite
- **Independent**: 5.1 (core), 5.3 (ratelimit), 5.4 (store), 5.5 (telemetry), 5.6 (bootstrap), 5.7 (oauth), 5.8 (webhook)
- **Depends on 5.1**: 5.2 (sdk) — must complete first since SDK wraps core
- All 8 can run in 2 rounds: round 1 = 5.1 + {5.3-5.8}; round 2 = 5.2
- **Blast radius**: Low. Each crate is independently compilable. Failure in one does not affect others.

### Phase 4 — Infra (6.x): 7 crates
- **Independent**: 6.1 (streaming), 6.2 (graphql), 6.3 (tailscale), 6.4 (host), 6.5 (mesh), 6.6 (registry), 6.7 (sandbox)
- All 7 can run in parallel after 2.2 + 3.3 complete.
- **Blast radius**: Medium. fcp-streaming and fcp-graphql are load-bearing for connectors. Test thoroughly.

### Phase 5 — Test Harness (7.x): 4 crates
- **Independent**: 7.1 (testkit), 7.2 (conformance), 7.3 (e2e), 7.4 (cli)
- All 4 can run in parallel after 2.3 completes.
- **Blast radius**: Low for individual crates, but 7.5 (cross-workspace E2E) must verify full integration.

### Phase 6 — Connector Wave A (8.x): 21 connectors
- All independent of each other. All depend on 5.2 (SDK migration).
- Can run highly parallel (limited only by agent capacity).
- **Blast radius**: Low per-connector. Each is isolated.

### Phase 7 — Connector Wave B (9.x): 6 streaming connectors
- All depend on 6.1 (streaming) + 6.2 (graphql) + 5.2 (SDK).
- Can run parallel within the wave.
- **Blast radius**: Higher due to bidirectional streaming complexity. Discord and Telegram are the most complex.

## Risk Controls

### Rollback Boundaries
- **Per-crate**: Each migration is an independent commit. Revert = git revert on that commit.
- **Per-wave**: If a wave gate fails, all crates in that wave can be reverted independently.
- **Substrate**: 2.1/2.2 is the highest-risk change. Requires full workspace `cargo check` + `cargo test` via rch before merge.

### Validation Checkpoints
- **Pre-wave gate**: rch cargo check -p <crate> must pass
- **Post-crate gate**: rch cargo test -p <crate> must pass
- **Post-wave gate**: rch cargo test --workspace must pass + invariant count check
- **Final gate**: Zero-exception ledger closure (10.3) requires all invariants at 0

### Non-Parallelizable Bottlenecks
1. **2.1 → 2.2**: Substrate integration must fully complete before API surface cleanup
2. **5.2 (fcp-sdk)**: Blocks all 21 Wave A connectors and all 6 Wave B connectors
3. **10.x**: Strictly serial — remove deps, verify, sign off

### Merge-Safety Requirements
- All changes on main branch (no feature branches per AGENTS.md)
- Commits must not increase any source_invariant count (ledger ratchet enforces this)
- Each migration commit must be self-contained: one crate, one commit

## Estimated Throughput
- With 3-5 parallel agents: ~3-4 days for core+infra waves, ~2-3 days for connector waves
- Critical path limiting factor: Phase 2 substrate (2.1 + 2.2) — ~1-2 days
- Total: ~8-10 days from substrate start to final cutover

—SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:22:09Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-1ud0u.10.4","title":"[ASUPERSYNC][Final] User-journey E2E replay + observability signoff","description":"## Background
Technical migration completion is insufficient unless representative user journeys continue to behave correctly with clear, trustworthy diagnostics.

## Objective
Execute final user-journey E2E replays and observability signoff across migrated connector/runtime surfaces.

## Work Plan
- Define representative end-user scenarios spanning request/response and streaming connectors.
- Replay scenarios under normal, edge, and failure conditions (timeouts, retries, cancellation, reconnect).
- Validate output semantics, error surfaces, and latency behavior against expected contracts.
- Review logs/traces for completeness, ordering clarity, and secret-safe output.

## Validation
- rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-e2e -- --nocapture
- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace -- --nocapture (targeted user-journey matrix)
